name: approvals
description: The multi-party approval workflow engine. Every action across the platform that needs 'N humans must approve before this executes' traverses this module — from access grants (via access/requests) to money movements (refunds >$500), configuration changes, sensitive data access, platform-staff impersonation session extensions. Ships the `#[AsApprovableAction]` attribute for domain modules to decorate their actions, the `approve` middleware that intercepts execution and routes through the engine, the `symfony/expression-language`-backed rule engine that evaluates template `when_expression` conditions + approver selectors, seven owned tables that persist templates + instances + decisions, sequential + parallel approver groups via `sort_order` on template_approvers, OOF-aware approver resolution via access/delegation, quorum evaluation per requirement + AND-across-requirements per instance, recall/withdraw before decision, hard timeout via `expires_at` + configurable `on_timeout` behavior (expire vs approve vs escalate), automated reminders per approver, comprehensive audit trail. Templates are RUNTIME DATA — tenant admins author + version them via the workflow-approval-templates surface without deployment. This is the load-bearing module for governance: SOC 2 + ISO 27001 + PCI-DSS access-review controls all rely on it. Rejected: `#[RequiresApproval(template:, when:)]` static attribute (D-A4 — templates as code would require deploy to change approver rules). Rejected: `HasApprovals` trait intercepting `__invoke()` (D-A5 — PHP mechanics don't support it; middleware is the seam). Rejected: json-logic-php + custom DSL + OPA/Cerbos (D-A6 — expression-language is PHP-native + sandbox-friendly + cache-compilable). Rejected: single-JSON approver-groups column (D-A7 — normalized `approval_template_approvers` table keeps quorum evaluation SQL-queryable).

consumedIndirectlyBy.description: Every module that has an action requiring multi-party approval decorates the action with `#[AsApprovableAction('<action_key>')]`. The `approve` middleware intercepts, resolves a template, and defers execution. Consumers NEVER inject this module's services directly — they compose via the attribute + middleware seam.
modules: compliance (data export approvals, retention override approvals)
tenants (tenant creation on regulated business_type, provider verification)
notifications (bulk unsubscribe overrides, per-tenant channel disabling)

wraps.rationale: Symfony's ExpressionLanguage gives us a battle-tested, cache-compilable, sandbox-safe expression grammar out of the box. We WRAP it in `ExpressionLanguageAdapter` which (a) registers the custom selector functions (`role()`, `user()`, `permission()`, `manager_of()`, `owner_of()`, `any()`, `all()`, `except()`), (b) enforces a hard 250ms per-evaluation timeout via a wall-clock deadline check, (c) caches compiled expression ASTs in Redis keyed on the expression source SHA-256, (d) captures parse failures + runtime failures as `ApproverSelectorEvaluationFailed` events instead of letting them propagate as uncaught PHP exceptions. Tenant admins cannot ship arbitrary PHP through the field — the grammar is closed.

tenant.description: Tenant-facing approval-engine surface. Every route below carries the `sanctum` guard + `X-Application-Id` header. Two sub-audiences: (1) tenant admins who author + manage approval templates, (2) every tenant user who is a requester or approver on individual instances.

platform-admin.description: Platform-admin cross-tenant read + approvable-action registry management. Compliance-report generation (SOC 2 CC6.3 + ISO 27001 A.9.2 evidence). Never mutates tenant approval decisions — that stays on the tenant plane.

planned_consumers: every domain module decorating an action with #[AsApprovableAction]
